subject: [PATCH v1 13/17] fuse: add passthrough support for atomic file creation

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH needs to be set in ff->open_flags to allow finish_open to open the passthrough file with the correct RW mode.
reviews.raw_body:
Unless I am missing something, you need to set FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH
in ff->open_flags otherwise finish_open will not open the ff->passthrough
file (with the correct RW mode).

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that enforcing FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH and backing id in FUSE_OPEN/FUSE_CREATE reply is overkill, agreed to remove this restriction in v2
reviews.raw_body:
Currently in this series, the kernel enforces that if there is a
backing file registered for the inode, the server is still expected to
set FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH and provide the backing id in the FUSE_OPEN /
FUSE_CREATE reply. My original motivation was for keeping the api
contract consistent between FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO and the regular
passthrough mode, but I think you're right that this is just overkill.
I'll remove this restriction in v2.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer Amir Goldstein initially misinterpreted the patch's subject and thought it was about passing through the CREATE operation, but then clarified that this comment is irrelevant to the current patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Actually, that's not what I meant.
I had a silly braino reading the subject
"add passthrough support for atomic file creation" and thinking that we want
to passthrough the CREATE op :)
so you may ignore this comment in the scope of this patch.

As for the api, I am not sure if it's an overkill.
I kind of like it the way it is - IMO keeping the dialect over explicit
is better than being ambiguous, but am open to other opinions.

Thanks,
Amir.

2026-04-20.patch_summary: This patch adds support for atomic file creation through the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) interface. It allows the server to return a backing ID, which is used to associate the newly created inode with the backing file for passthrough operations before the dentry is instantiated. The patch modifies the fuse_create_open function to use either the original entry_out structure or the new entry2_out structure depending on whether FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO is enabled. If entry2_out is used, it processes the server's response and extracts the backing ID. This change enables atomic file creation for FUSE filesystems that support passthrough operations.
